name: Milvus
shortDescription: Connect FlowRunner to [Milvus](https://milvus.io) / [Zilliz Cloud](https://zilliz.com/cloud) — an open-source vector database for similarity search and AI applications. Manage collections, write and delete entities, run vector similarity search, and administer indexes and partitions through the Milvus REST API v2.

actions.name: Create Collection
actions.description: Creates a new collection. In quick-setup mode, supply a Dimension and Metric Type to auto-generate a schema with an auto-generated primary key and a single float-vector field. For full control, provide a custom Schema object (fields definition) and optionally override the primary/vector field names, ID type and auto-ID behavior. A collection must be loaded before it can be searched or queried.

actions.name: List Collections
actions.description: Retrieves the names of all collections in the current database.

actions.name: Describe Collection
actions.description: Retrieves the full definition of a collection, including its schema (fields and data types), configured indexes, load status, aliases and consistency level.

actions.name: Drop Collection
actions.description: Permanently deletes a collection along with all entities and indexes it contains. This action cannot be undone.

actions.name: Has Collection
actions.description: Checks whether a collection with the given name exists in the current database. Returns an object with a "has" boolean.

actions.name: Get Collection Stats
actions.description: Retrieves statistics for a collection, most notably its total entity (row) count.

actions.name: Load Collection
actions.description: Loads a collection (and its index) into memory so it can serve search and query requests. A collection must be loaded before Search or Query will return results. Loading is asynchronous; large collections may take time to become fully loaded.

actions.name: Release Collection
actions.description: Releases a collection from memory to free resources. Once released, the collection can no longer serve search or query requests until it is loaded again.

actions.name: Insert Entities
actions.description: Inserts one or more entities (rows) into a collection. Each entity is an object whose keys match the collection's field names, e.g. {"id":1,"vector":[0.1,0.2,...],"title":"Doc"}. Omit the primary key when the collection uses auto-ID. Returns the count of inserted rows and their primary keys.

actions.name: Upsert Entities
actions.description: Inserts new entities or replaces existing ones matched by primary key in a collection. Each entity is an object whose keys match the collection's field names. Use this to write data idempotently without checking for existence first.

actions.name: Delete Entities
actions.description: Deletes entities from a collection by an explicit list of primary key IDs or by a boolean filter expression. Provide exactly one selector. Filter expressions use Milvus syntax, e.g. "color in [\"red\",\"green\"]" or "id > 100".

actions.name: Search Entities
actions.description: Performs a vector similarity search — the primary nearest-neighbor operation. Supply one or more query vectors and Milvus returns the closest entities ranked by distance/similarity. Supports scalar filtering, output field selection, pagination via offset, and per-request search parameters (metric type and index params like nprobe/ef). The collection must be loaded first (use Load Collection).

actions.name: Query Entities
actions.description: Retrieves entities that match a scalar boolean filter expression, without vector similarity. Use it to fetch rows by metadata, e.g. "color == \"red\" and price < 100". Supports selecting output fields and limiting the number of rows. The collection must be loaded first.

actions.name: Get Entities
actions.description: Retrieves entities from a collection by their primary key IDs. Optionally restricts the returned fields. The collection must be loaded first.

actions.name: Create Index
actions.description: Creates one or more indexes on fields of a collection. Each index entry names the target field, the index type (e.g. AUTOINDEX, IVF_FLAT, HNSW) and, for vector fields, the distance metric. Building an index accelerates vector search and scalar filtering.

actions.name: Describe Index
actions.description: Retrieves the configuration and build state of a specific index on a collection, including its index type, metric type, parameters and indexed-rows count.

actions.name: List Indexes
actions.description: Retrieves the names of all indexes defined on a collection.

actions.name: Drop Index
actions.description: Permanently deletes an index from a collection. The collection must be released from memory before an index can be dropped.

actions.name: List Partitions
actions.description: Retrieves the names of all partitions within a collection.

actions.name: Create Partition
actions.description: Creates a new partition within a collection. Partitions let you group entities so that searches and inserts can be scoped to a subset of the data for better performance.

actions.name: Drop Partition
actions.description: Permanently deletes a partition and all entities it contains from a collection. The partition must be released from memory before it can be dropped. This action cannot be undone.

idealUseCases: Build a semantic search or retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) backend by storing embeddings and
Sync application data into a vector collection, keeping it current with insert, upsert, and delete
Filter and retrieve records by metadata (scalar fields) without vector similarity, or fetch
Administer a vector database — create and drop collections, indexes, and partitions, and manage

On a user query, generate a query vector with **OpenAI** "Create Embeddings" and pass it to
